004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.microservice.jetty;
018
019import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
020import org.apache.juneau.microservice.*;
021
022import jakarta.servlet.*;
023
024/**
025 * Zero-config convenience launcher for a Jetty-backed Juneau microservice.
026 *
027 * <p>
028 * Wraps the {@link Microservice} + {@link JettyConfiguration} bootstrap in a single static call so
029 * a consumer can stand up a Jetty microservice with one Maven dependency and one line of code:
030 *
031 * <p class='bjava'>
032 *    <jc>// Boot Jetty + Juneau on the default port (10000) with the interactive console enabled.</jc>
033 *    <jk>public static void</jk> main(String[] args) <jk>throws</jk> Exception {
034 *       JettyMicroservice.<jsm>run</jsm>(args, <jk>new</jk> RootResources());
035 *    }
036 * </p>
037 *
038 * <p>
039 * Bundled defaults (all overridable):
040 * <ul>
041 *    <li>Port <c>10000</c> (surfaced as <c>Jetty/port</c> in the bundled <c>juneau.cfg</c>).
042 *    <li>Interactive console enabled (use {@link #run(String[], Servlet, boolean)} with
043 *       {@code startConsole=false} to suppress).
044 *    <li>Classpath defaults for <c>jetty.xml</c> and <c>juneau.cfg</c> &mdash; a consumer-supplied
045 *       copy on the working-directory or classpath wins via the existing
046 *       {@link org.apache.juneau.microservice.Microservice} resolution chain.
047 * </ul>
048 *
049 * <p>
050 * The supplied root servlet is registered as a {@code @Bean Servlet} in an external bean store and
051 * auto-mounted by {@link org.apache.juneau.microservice.jetty.JettyServerComponent JettyServerComponent}
052 * at the path declared by its {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.Rest @Rest} annotation.  Consumers
053 * who want full control over the bean store / configuration classes / listener wiring should call
054 * {@link Microservice#create()} directly &mdash; this facade is a thin convenience over that builder.
055 *
056 * @since 10.0.0
057 */
058@SuppressWarnings({
059   "resource" // beanStore lifetime is managed by the returned Microservice; not an independent resource.
060})
061public final class JettyMicroservice {
062
063   private JettyMicroservice() {}
064
065   /**
066    * Boots a Jetty microservice with the supplied root servlet and the bundled defaults,
067    * with the interactive console enabled.
068    *
069    * <p>
070    * Equivalent to calling {@link #run(String[], Servlet, boolean) run(args, rootServlet, true)}.
071    *
072    * @param args         The {@code main(String[])} arguments.  Forwarded to {@link org.apache.juneau.microservice.Microservice.Builder#args(String...)}.
073    * @param rootServlet  The root REST servlet (typically annotated with
074    *                     {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.Rest @Rest}).
075    * @return The started {@link Microservice} instance.  Callers typically chain {@link Microservice#join()}.
076    * @throws Exception Error occurred during bootstrap.
077    */
078   @SuppressWarnings({
079      "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - mirrors Microservice.start() lifecycle contract.
080   })
081   public static Microservice run(String[] args, Servlet rootServlet) throws Exception {
082      return run(args, rootServlet, true);
083   }
084
085   /**
086    * Boots a Jetty microservice with the supplied root servlet and the bundled defaults.
087    *
088    * <p>
089    * Constructs the {@link Microservice}, registers the supplied servlet as a {@code @Bean Servlet} so
090    * {@link org.apache.juneau.microservice.jetty.JettyServerComponent JettyServerComponent} auto-mounts
091    * it, applies {@link JettyConfiguration} so Jetty itself is wired, and starts the lifecycle.  The
092    * returned microservice has not yet been {@link Microservice#join() joined}; callers wanting the
093    * standard "start and block forever" loop should chain {@code .join()}.
094    *
095    * @param args          The {@code main(String[])} arguments.  Forwarded to {@link org.apache.juneau.microservice.Microservice.Builder#args(String...)}.
096    * @param rootServlet   The root REST servlet (typically annotated with
097    *                      {@link org.apache.juneau.rest.server.Rest @Rest}).
098    * @param startConsole  If <jk>true</jk>, also starts the interactive console thread via
099    *                      {@link Microservice#startConsole()}.
100    * @return The started {@link Microservice} instance.
101    * @throws Exception Error occurred during bootstrap.
102    */
103   @SuppressWarnings({
104      "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - mirrors Microservice.start() lifecycle contract.
105   })
106   public static Microservice run(String[] args, Servlet rootServlet, boolean startConsole) throws Exception {
107      var beanStore = new BasicBeanStore();
108      beanStore.addBean(Servlet.class, rootServlet);
109      return run(args, beanStore, startConsole, JettyConfiguration.class);
110   }
111
112   /**
113    * Power-user form: boots a Jetty microservice with a caller-supplied bean store and configuration
114    * class list.
115    *
116    * <p>
117    * Equivalent to writing the {@link Microservice} builder chain by hand, but with the boilerplate
118    * collapsed.  Use this when the simpler {@link #run(String[], Servlet)} overloads are too
119    * restrictive &mdash; e.g. multiple {@code @Bean Servlet} contributions, custom
120    * {@code MicroserviceListener}s, or a parent bean store.
121    *
122    * @param args            The {@code main(String[])} arguments.  Forwarded to {@link org.apache.juneau.microservice.Microservice.Builder#args(String...)}.
123    * @param beanStore       The bean store to use for dependency injection.  Pre-populated bean entries
124    *                        ({@code @Bean Servlet}, {@code MicroserviceListener}, etc.) are picked up by
125    *                        {@link JettyConfiguration} and the microservice runtime.
126    * @param startConsole    If <jk>true</jk>, also starts the interactive console thread.
127    * @param configurations  The {@code @Configuration}-annotated classes to register.  Always includes
128    *                        {@link JettyConfiguration}; pass an empty list to use only that.
129    * @return The started {@link Microservice} instance.
130    * @throws Exception Error occurred during bootstrap.
131    */
132   @SuppressWarnings({
133      "java:S112" // throws Exception intentional - mirrors Microservice.start() lifecycle contract.
134   })
135   public static Microservice run(String[] args, WritableBeanStore beanStore, boolean startConsole, Class<?>... configurations) throws Exception {
136      var ms = Microservice
137         .create()
138         .args(args)
139         .beanStore(beanStore)
140         .configurations(configurations)
141         .build()
142         .start();
143      if (startConsole)
144         ms.startConsole();
145      return ms;
146   }
147}
